name: Nard
author: Abstract Games factory
description: Nard (Long Nardy): the Persian tables race game. All 15 of your checkers start stacked on your 'head' in diagonally opposite corners, and BOTH players run them the same way around the 24-point board into their home quadrant, then bear them off; first to bear off all 15 wins. Unlike backgammon there is NO hitting — a checker may never land on any point holding an opposing checker, so there are no blots, no bar and no re-entry, making it a pure race-and-block contest. Only one checker may leave the head each turn (two on your first turn). Roll two dice; doubles move four times; you must use both dice if you legally can. (No doubling cube.)
